fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: South Dakota CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to South Dakota - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity south dakota, how to transfer CNA license to south dakota, south dakota CNA endorsement application

board.name: South Dakota DOH
board.mailing_address: 4305 S. Louise Ave., Suite 201, Sioux Falls, SD 57106-3118

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in South Dakota.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you meet the South Dakota requirements for CNA registration, which typically involves completing a state-approved nurse aide training program and passing the competency evaluation, or qualifying for endorsement from another state.

steps.title: Download Application Packet
steps.description: Visit the South Dakota Board of Nursing's Nurse Aide Registry Information page (bon.sd.gov/Nurse-Aide-Registry-Information.aspx) and download the appropriate 'Nurse Aide Registry Application Packet' (Initial or Endorsement).

steps.title: Complete Application Form
steps.description: Fill out the downloaded application packet completely and accurately. Ensure all sections, including personal information, education/training details, and eligibility questions, are answered. Remember to sign the application.

steps.title: Gather Supporting Documents
steps.description: Collect all required documentation as specified in the application packet. This may include proof of training, competency evaluation results, verification from other states (for endorsement), and any other requested materials.

steps.title: Submit Application by Mail
steps.description: Mail the original signed application packet along with all required supporting documents to the South Dakota Board of Nursing at the address provided in the packet and on their contact page.

steps.title: Receive Registry Confirmation
steps.description: Allow the Board time to process your application. Once approved, your name will be added to the South Dakota Nurse Aide Registry, and you can verify your status via the online verification portal.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does South Dakota CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: There is no application fee for CNA endorsement in South Dakota. However, background check fees may apply.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in South Dakota.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to South Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in South Dakota while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in SD.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to South Dakota.

hero.title: Become a CNA in South Dakota
hero.dek: To apply for initial CNA registration or endorsement in South Dakota, download and complete the official application packet from the South Dakota Board of Nursing Nurse Aide Registry Forms page and submit it via mail.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in South Dakota
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to South Dakota CNA certification.

official_links.portal_name: South Dakota Board of Nursing Nurse Aide Registry Forms Page
official_links.registration_steps: Download the appropriate application packet (initial or endorsement) from the SD Board of Nursing's Nurse Aide Registry Information page, complete it thoroughly, gather all required supporting documents, and mail the original signed application to the Board of Nursing.
